jira.description: Jira issue tracking — search, create, update, comment, transition issues

omega-memory.description: Persistent agent memory with semantic search, multi-agent coordination, and knowledge graphs — run via uvx (richer than the basic memory store)

longhand.description: Lossless agent session history — indexes raw tool calls, file edits, and thinking blocks. Complements memory/omega-memory (synthesized) with verbatim recall. Install: pip install longhand && longhand setup

exa-web-search.description: Web search, research, and data ingestion via Exa API — prefer task-scoped use for broader research after GitHub search and primary docs

context7.description: Live documentation lookup — use with /docs command and documentation-lookup skill (resolve-library-id, query-docs).

playwright.description: Browser automation and testing via Playwright

fal-ai.description: AI image/video/audio generation via fal.ai models

browser-use.description: AI browser agent for web tasks

devfleet.description: Multi-agent orchestration — dispatch parallel agents in isolated worktrees. Plan projects, auto-chain missions, and read structured reports. Repo: https://github.com/LEC-AI/claude-devfleet

token-optimizer.description: Token optimization for 95%+ context reduction via content deduplication and compression

laraplugins.description: Laravel plugin discovery — search packages by keyword, health score, Laravel/PHP version compatibility. Use with laravel-plugin-discovery skill.

confluence.description: Confluence Cloud integration — search pages, retrieve content, explore spaces

evalview.description: AI agent regression testing — snapshot behavior, detect regressions in tool calls and output quality. 8 tools: create_test, run_snapshot, run_check, list_tests, validate_skill, generate_skill_tests, run_skill_test, generate_visual_report. API key optional — deterministic checks (tool diff, output hash) work without it. Install: pip install "evalview>=0.5,<1"

serena.description: IDE assistant MCP server providing context integration for AI agents and editors

squish.description: Local-first persistent memory runtime for AI agents — MCP server for pi, Cursor, OpenCode, Codex, Cline. Auto-captures context across sessions. 1-20ms recall, 283KB, no second LLM needed. Runs locally with SQLite. Supports cloud sync via Stripe checkout ($9-$99/mo). GitHub: https://github.com/michielhdoteth/squish | Docs: https://squishplugin.dev | (also available via local `squish run mcp`)

_comments.usage: Apply a named template from mcp-configs/templates/ or copy the servers you need into a pi mcp.json file
_comments.env_vars: Templates use ${VAR} references resolved from the process environment at runtime; do not put real tokens in this file
_comments.templates: Optional additive templates currently cover memory, reasoning, and research under mcp-configs/templates/
_comments.disabling: Disable bundled MCPs with disabledServers in ~/.pi/agent/mcp.json, or override them by defining the same server name in .pi/mcp.json or ~/.pi/agent/mcp.json
_comments.context_warning: Keep under 10 MCPs enabled to preserve context window
